name: Camp Dubois
desc: Mouth of the Missouri River — expedition begins

name: Fort Osage
desc: First major American fort on the Missouri

name: Council Bluffs
desc: Council with the Otoe-Missouria people

name: Calumet Bluff
desc: Spirit Mound — Sioux territory begins

name: Fort Mandan
desc: Winter quarters — Sacagawea joins here

name: Yellowstone Jct.
desc: Confluence of the Yellowstone River

name: Great Falls
desc: Five cataracts — an 18-mile portage

name: Lemhi Pass
desc: Continental Divide — the Shoshone homeland

name: Travelers' Rest
desc: Nez Perce country — last rest before Columbia

name: Fort Clatsop
desc: Pacific Ocean — the goal of the expedition

name: Fort Leavenworth
desc: Kansas garrison — soldiers warn of Comanche to the south

name: Platte River Fork
desc: The Platte runs west — wide and shallow, easy going

name: Grand Island
desc: A large island — Pawnee hunting grounds

name: Fort Atkinson
desc: Army outpost — soldiers have heard of mountains ahead

name: Spanish Borderlands
desc: Spanish patrols challenge your right to be here

name: Pembina Post
desc: British-Canadian fur trade post — friendly but far north

name: Assiniboine River
desc: The great northern river — leads into British Rupert's Land

name: British Territory
desc: You have crossed into territory claimed by the Crown

name: Arkansas Headwaters
desc: Pike's country — the mountains loom but no pass is known

name: Spanish Pass
desc: A Spanish mule path — leads south into New Mexico, not west
